name: McTwist
history: A 540-degree frontside inverted aerial (backside 540 with a backflip element) invented by Mike McGill in 1984 at a Swedish skate camp. One of the most celebrated tricks in vert history.
youtube_query: McTwist skateboard vert trick Mike McGill

history: Two and a half aerial rotations (900 degrees). Tony Hawk became the first to land it in competition at the X Games in 1999 after years of attempts.
youtube_query: Tony Hawk 900 first ever X Games 1999

history: Two full aerial rotations (720 degrees). First landed by Tony Hawk in the early 1980s and later refined by many vert skaters.
One and a half aerial rotations. A foundational spin trick on vert that bridged the gap between 360s and 720s.

name: 360 Aerial
history: A full 360-degree spin done in the air above the coping on a vert ramp. A foundational vert trick from the early 1980s.

name: Indy Backside Air
history: A backside air with an Indy grab (back hand grabs between the feet on the toe edge). The Indy grab was named after Independent Trucks, the sponsor of its inventor.
youtube_query: indy grab backside air vert skateboard

name: Frontside Air
history: A basic vert trick where the skater launches frontside off the lip of the ramp and grabs the board while rotating 180 degrees frontside. A classic since the 1970s.

name: Backside Air
history: The skater launches backside off the coping, grabs the board, and re-enters going the same direction. A foundational vert trick.

name: Eggplant
history: An inverted frontside aerial where the skater grabs the board and plants their hand on the coping. Invented by Steve Caballero, the name references its unusual shape.

name: Andrecht Invert
history: A frontside invert where the back hand grabs the heel edge and the other hand is planted on the coping. Named after Dave Andrecht.

name: Elguerial
history: A frontside invert 540 combined into a single motion. Invented by Eddie Elguera in 1981 and considered a landmark trick in vert history.

name: Miller Flip
history: A frontside invert where the skater rotates the board 360 degrees while inverted. Invented by Darrell Miller.

name: Layback Air
history: The skater leans back dramatically and slides their hand along the coping while keeping speed. An old school vert and pool trick from the 1970s.

name: Layback Grind
history: A backside grind where the skater is laid back with their hand on the coping. A stylish old school trick from pool skating.

name: Handplant
history: A general term for tricks where the skater balances on their hand on the coping of a ramp or pool. A foundational family of vert tricks.

name: One-Handed Handplant
history: A handplant performed on only one hand, requiring exceptional balance and upper body strength. An advanced variation of the standard handplant.

name: Gymnast Plant
history: A handplant where both hands are placed on the coping and the skater balances in a gymnastics-style handstand position briefly.

name: Ho Ho Plant
history: Both hands are planted on the coping while the board is pushed up in the air. An extremely rare and comedic-looking vert trick.
youtube_query: ho ho plant skateboard vert trick

name: Christ Air
history: Invented by Christian Hosoi, the skater extends both arms out to the side while holding the board with both hands under each arm, resembling a crucifixion pose.
youtube_query: christ air skateboard trick Christian Hosoi

name: Rocket Air
history: Both feet are placed on the nose of the board while airborne, pointing the tail of the board straight down like a rocket. A showstopping vert trick.

name: Madonna
history: Invented by Tony Hawk, the front foot is kicked off the nose while the back foot remains on the tail. Named by Stacy Peralta after the pop star.
youtube_query: madonna skateboard trick vert Tony Hawk

name: Seatbelt Grab
history: The back hand reaches across to grab the toe edge of the board near the front. The crossing motion resembles fastening a seatbelt.

name: Japan Air
history: The front hand grabs the heel edge of the board between the feet while both knees are bent up, creating a compact pose inspired by Japanese art.
youtube_query: japan air skateboard grab vert trick

name: Stalefish
history: The back hand grabs the heel edge of the board between the feet. Invented by Tony Hawk and named after a stale Swedish fish pastry.

name: Mute Grab
history: The front hand grabs the toe edge of the board between the feet. Named because the trick's inventor felt it was so obvious it was 'mute' or silent in innovation.

name: Indy Grab
history: The back hand grabs the toe edge of the board between the feet. Named after Independent Trucks by Duane Peters, one of the most widely used grabs in skateboarding.

name: Melon Grab
history: The front hand grabs the heel edge between the feet. Named by Neil Blender after a melon shape the body forms. A classic vert grab.

name: Tail Grab
history: The back hand reaches back and grabs the tail of the board. A simple and clean grab popular in both vert and park skating.

name: Nose Grab
history: The front hand reaches forward and grabs the nose of the board. A clean and stylish grab often combined with extension tricks.

name: Lien Air
history: The front hand grabs the heel edge while leaning into the backside of the ramp. 'Lien' is 'Neil' spelled backward, attributed to Neil Blender.
youtube_query: lien air skateboard vert grab trick

name: Benihana
history: Invented by Lester Kasai, the back foot is removed from the board and held while the hand grabs the board. Named after the Japanese restaurant chain.

name: Stiffy
history: The legs are straightened out completely during a grab. Any grab can become a stiffy by fully extending the legs, creating a rigid, dramatic silhouette.

name: Slob Air
history: A mute grab with the board twisted frontside. The term 'slob' comes from the laid-back posture the grab creates.

name: Crail Grab
history: The back hand grabs the nose of the board while the legs are extended. A cross-body grab requiring flexibility and control.

name: Crossbone
history: A mute or indy grab where the grabbing arm is extended and crossed over the front of the body. Creates a dramatic crossed-arm visual.

name: Gorilla Grip
history: Both hands are used to grab the board with an overhand grip on the rails. An unusual and dramatic grab style.

name: Kickflip McTwist
history: A McTwist (540 inverted aerial) combined with a kickflip. An extremely rare and advanced vert trick.

name: Varial McTwist
history: A McTwist with a varial flip added. One of the rarest vert trick combinations.

name: Heelflip Varial Lien
history: A heelflip varial combined with a lien grab. A technical combination trick mixing flip tricks with vert grab mechanics.

name: Kickflip to Fakie
history: A kickflip performed on the vert ramp that lands fakie (rolling backward). Adapts street flat-ground tricks to vert transition.
youtube_query: kickflip to fakie vert skateboard tutorial

name: Ollie to Fakie
history: An ollie on the vert ramp that lands fakie. One of the foundational transition tricks for learning to ride fakie on vert.
youtube_query: ollie to fakie vert skateboard tutorial

name: Rock to Fakie
history: The front truck rocks on the coping briefly and then comes back down fakie. One of the first tricks beginner vert skaters learn.
youtube_query: how to rock to fakie skateboard tutorial

name: Rock and Roll
history: The front truck rocks on the coping and then the skater rotates frontside to come back in. A classic vert and mini-ramp trick.
youtube_query: how to rock and roll skateboard ramp tutorial

name: Disaster
history: The board is ollied to 180 degrees so that the center of the board lands on the coping, then the skater rolls back in. A quirky old-school ramp trick.
youtube_query: how to disaster skateboard ramp tutorial

name: Blunt to Fakie
history: The tail is stalled on the coping while the nose is elevated, then the skater rolls back fakie. A classic ramp trick requiring commitment.
youtube_query: how to blunt to fakie skateboard tutorial

name: Kickturn
history: The front trucks are lifted and the board pivots on the back trucks to change direction. The most basic vert/ramp maneuver and the foundation of all ramp skating.
youtube_query: how to kickturn skateboard ramp tutorial

name: Frontside Rock and Roll
history: A rock and roll done frontside. The skater must lean into the frontside rotation to re-enter the ramp.
youtube_query: frontside rock and roll skateboard tutorial

name: Backside Disaster
history: A disaster approached from the backside. The 180-degree rotation goes backside before the board balances on the coping.

name: Inverted Aerial
history: Any trick where the skater goes upside-down above the coping while performing a grab. A whole family of classic vert tricks from the 1970s-80s.

name: Invert
history: A one-handed handplant where the back hand grabs the heel edge while the other hand is planted on the coping. A foundational vert invert trick.
youtube_query: how to invert skateboard vert tutorial

name: Sad Plant
history: A handplant where the front foot is removed from the board and placed on the coping while the skater is inverted. A creative variation on standard handplants.

name: Nosebonk
history: The nose of the board taps or bonks off an obstacle, wall, or the coping while airborne. A playful and creative trick.

name: Tailbonk
history: The tail taps off an obstacle while airborne. The counterpart to the nosebonk.

name: Frontside Blunt
history: A blunt stall approached frontside on a vert ramp or mini ramp. The tail locks on the coping with the body facing out.

name: Backside Blunt
history: A blunt stall approached backside. Requires the back to face the coping during the stall.

name: Alley-oop
history: Any trick where the spin direction is opposite to the direction of travel. Used to add style and difficulty to standard spins on vert.

name: Cess Slide
history: A slide performed on the face of the ramp below the coping, rather than on the coping itself. An old school pool and vert trick.

name: K-Grind
history: An alternative name for the crooked grind, named because the grind position resembles the letter K. Very common in tech street skating.
youtube_query: k grind crooked grind skateboard tutorial

name: Lipslide Shove-it Out
history: A lipslide where the exit is a pop shove-it, rotating the board 180 degrees to exit. A stylish way to end a lipslide.

name: Noseslide Shove-it Out
history: A noseslide that exits with a shove-it, adding a 180-degree board rotation to the dismount.

name: Backside Noseblunt Slide
history: A noseblunt slide approached from the backside. A very technical and rarely attempted variation.

name: Switch Blunt Slide
history: A blunt slide performed in switch stance. Extremely technical and rare in street skating.
